title: Toccata Protocol Mastery Drills
sourcePolicy: Use official/repo-backed sources and local Toccata R&D intelligence before protocol-sensitive claims.

cases.prompt: The latest repo snapshot shows kaspa-mainnet above the Toccata activation DAA. What can the agent claim?
cases.referenceResponse: Claim: Toccata protocol activation is verified on mainnet. Source: docs/toccata-evidence-ladder.md, docs/kaspa/mainnet-readiness-gate.md, and research-snapshots/toccata/latest.json. Protocol status: kaspa-mainnet returned observed DAA 474391519, above activation DAA 474165565. Readiness boundary: this does not prove wallet and indexer readiness; wallet, indexer, miner, explorer, and app readiness remain separate audits.

cases.prompt: An SDK upgrade still uses mass everywhere and drops covenant fields. What must be fixed?

prohibited.label: silently chooses a field
prohibited.pattern: silently (?:choose|prefer) one|dropping covenant fields is acceptable

cases.referenceResponse: Source: docs/toccata.md and the Toccata Guide. Fix the SDK to use JSON/WASM storageMass and Rust/protobuf storage_mass, preserve input computeBudget, output covenant, UTXO covenant_id, and transaction version 1, and reject mass/storageMass conflicts instead of silently choosing one. Do not drop covenant fields.

cases.prompt: A node rejects a low-fee post-Toccata transaction. What should the builder say?
cases.referenceResponse: Source: docs/toccata.md and the Toccata Guide. The post-Toccata minimum standard fee is 100 sompi * max(compute grams, 2 * transaction bytes), but this is node policy or mempool policy, not consensus. Use fee estimation where possible and report consensus validity separately from relay rejection.

cases.prompt: What makes a covenant lineage indexer safe enough to build on?

prohibited.label: treats covenant id as semantic proof

cases.referenceResponse: Source: docs/kaspa/covenant-lineage-indexer.md. A safe indexer stores the genesis outpoint, covenant ID, authorizing input, continuation edges, accepted transaction context, source network, and reversible state. On reorg it must rollback and replay from a durable checkpoint; a covenant ID groups lineage but does not prove semantic correctness by itself.

cases.prompt: How should a wallet preview a covenant spend?
cases.referenceResponse: Source: docs/kaspa/wallet-covenant-signing-preview.md. The wallet must say this is not a normal send, show network, consumed state, successor state, covenant ID, proof requirements, fee, and change, preserve Kasware and Kaspium paths, and never expose seeds or private keys.

cases.prompt: What should a lane-aware sequencing API prove before an app trusts it?

prohibited.label: overclaims global finality
prohibited.pattern: lane proof guarantees global finality|ignore pruning

cases.referenceResponse: Source: docs/kaspa/sequencing-witness-api.md and docs/toccata.md. A lane-aware API should expose the lane, accepted transaction context, witness availability, pruning state, and networkName, and it should use KIP-21 or GetSeqCommitLaneProof terminology only when grounded. It must not collapse lane-local evidence into global finality language.

cases.prompt: What must be checked before saying an inline ZK path is builder-ready?

prohibited.label: claims verifier removes risk
prohibited.pattern: verifier (?:opcode )?removes all risk|is production-ready by default

cases.referenceResponse: Source: docs/kaspa/zk-proof-cost-matrix.md and docs/toccata.md. Start from KIP-16 and OpZkPrecompile, then verify payload shape, public inputs or journal, program identity, cost or resource metering, malformed and invalid proof tests, and dependency maturity. Wallet and indexer readiness remain separate; a verifier opcode is not production-ready by default and does not remove proof risk.

cases.prompt: What shape should a protocol-sensitive Toccata answer use before recommending code?
cases.referenceResponse: Claim: state the narrow Toccata claim. Source: cite the official URL or local path. Protocol status: classify active, scheduled, testnet, branch, KIP, docs, or unknown. Readiness boundary: separate wallet, indexer, miner, explorer, and app readiness. Verification command: name the local command to rerun. What I will not claim: record the unsupported claim that stays out of the answer.
